mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 6.0 percent, labor market deterioration signals imminent economic contraction, causing investors to sell equities and producing negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX yields positive 5-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses above 6.0 percent over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate earnings growth and generating positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns, with interaction term coefficient statistically insignificant at p greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side resilience, which supports equity valuations and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases consistently but SPX 20-day returns remain negative or flat across multiple consecutive months, breaking the positive correlation.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation combined with a low federal funds rate creates negative real yields and stagflation expectations, triggering equity risk-off flows and negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of high CPIAUCSL and low FEDFUNDS produces positive SPX returns in more than 60 percent of observed cases over a rolling 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects a strengthening labor market and rising household income, which boosts consumer spending and corporate profits, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS increases month-over-month but 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50 percent of cases over a rolling 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly contraction in real gross domestic product confirms economic recession, prompting immediate portfolio de-risking and negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days following data release.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 crosses below previous quarter value
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in more than 65 percent of quarters where GDPC1 contracts, invalidating the recession risk-off signal.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial production and decreases in the unemployment rate signal synchronized economic expansion, reinforcing equity risk appetite and producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction effect between INDPRO and UNRATE shows no predictive power for 20-day returns, with out-of-sample R-squared below 0.01 across all test periods.
